meta.purpose: Reasoning chains for open-source project adoption.
meta.load_condition: Load when user asks why projects fail to gain adoption.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Documentation depth helps existing users. Value-entry clarity creates new users. These are different functions.
logic: A visitor discovering a project does not read documentation — they scan for relevance.
If the value proposition is unclear in the first 30 seconds, the visitor leaves and never becomes a user.
Adding more documentation, tutorials, or examples helps users who have already committed — it does nothing for visitors who never committed.
Therefore, projects with low adoption should optimize value-entry clarity before documentation depth.
reasoning_chains.so_what: When diagnosing adoption problems, check the README first screen before checking code quality, test coverage, or documentation depth.
